title: Synthesis of Lightweight Aluminosilicate Proppants from Low-Grade Kaolinitic Clay And Industrial Waste

authors.name: Bayraeva Nasiba Ashurbayevna
authors.affiliation: Researcher, Institute of General and Inorganic Chemistry of Academy of Sciences of The Republic of Uzbekistan

authors.affiliation: Dsc, Institute of General and Inorganic Chemistry of Academy of Sciences of The Republic of Uzbekistan, Termez State University of Engineering and Agrotechnologies, Uzbekistan

authors.name: Abdurahimov Dilmurod Hayitmurotovich
authors.affiliation: PhD, Termez State University of Engineering and Agrotechnologies, Uzbekistan

abstract: <jats:p>This study presents the synthesis of lightweight aluminosilicate ceramic proppants using Angren kaolinitic clay and ash–slag industrial waste as raw materials. The effects of composition and sintering temperature (1200–1250 °C) on phase composition and physicomechanical properties were investigated. The results show that increasing the sintering temperature enhances densification, promotes mullite formation, and improves mechanical strength and chemical stability. The optimal composition (CA-3) exhibited low bulk density (1.43 g/cm³), low crushing ratio (6.58%), and high acid resistance (5.56%). XRD analysis confirmed increased crystallinity and mullite development at higher temperatures. The developed proppants demonstrate competitive properties and offer a cost-effective and environmentally sustainable alternative to conventional materials.</jats:p>
journal: The American Journal of Applied Sciences
